reasoning_chain_text: 1. Using the 2016, 2017, 2018, and 2019 summer meal program-period datasets, identify the top five Texas counties by lunch participation in each program year and keep the counties that appear in the top five in all four years.
2. Using the YE-2018 and YE-2020 day care home meal reimbursement datasets, compute the percentage change for those counties and keep the two with the largest drops.
3. Using the 2018 and 2019 child center meal reimbursement datasets, extract the enrollment measure for those two counties, compute the two-year average, and keep the higher one.

4. Using the county reference article, determine that county's county seat. Using the county-seat city reference article, identify the coast named there. Using the coast reference article, map that coast to the specific gulf to return.
original_final_question: I'm looking for a Texas county that consistently ranked in the top five for summer lunch participation from 2015 to 2019. Among those top counties, find the two that saw the largest percentage drops in day care home meal reimbursements from 2018-19 to 2020-21. Of those two, which one had higher child center enrollment on average from 2018-19 to 2019-20? Finally, identify the county seat of that county and tell me which specific gulf its coast borders. Please only report the name of the gulf in your answer. Your response should be in the format [Answer].

Node 2 (2016-2017): top five LunchTotal counties -> {Hidalgo, Harris, Dallas, Cameron, Bexar}
Node 3 (2017-2018): top five LunchTotal counties -> {Hidalgo, Harris, Dallas, Cameron, Bexar}
Node 4 (2018-2019): top five LunchTotal counties -> {Hidalgo, Harris, Dallas, Bexar, Cameron}
Intersection: {Harris, Hidalgo, Dallas, Cameron}
HOP 2 (d=2, CACFP day care homes reimbursements 2018-2019 vs 2020-2021 - nodes 5-6):
Percent decreases: Hidalgo -19.26%, Cameron -15.92%, Dallas -15.58%, Harris -13.69% -> two largest decreases {Hidalgo, Cameron}
HOP 3 (d=2, CACFP child centers EnrollmentQty 2018-2019 vs 2019-2020 - nodes 7-8):
Node 7 totals (2018-2019): Cameron 7,620,183; Hidalgo 1,661,128
Node 8 totals (2019-2020): Cameron 1,076,746; Hidalgo 1,477,805
Average EnrollmentQty: Cameron 4,348,464.5 vs Hidalgo 1,569,466.5 -> Cameron County
HOP 4 (d=1, Cameron County Wikipedia - node 9):
Node 9: Cameron County seat is Brownsville
HOP 5 (d=1, Brownsville Wikipedia - node 10):
Node 10: Brownsville is on the Gulf Coast
HOP 6 (d=1, Gulf Coast Wikipedia - node 11):
Node 11: Gulf Coast is the coastline along the Gulf of Mexico
Final answer: Gulf of Mexico
